entry.title: Re: Incorrect escape parameter for tag : DescriptionOfNat...
entry.content: Marc, It doesn't contain characters such as < or >. Contains - ; ‘ ’ ; ) ;(

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Create API User in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Create API User in SAP SuccessFactors

Note: Follow these steps only if you don't have an existing API user in SuccessFactors with the required permissions. 

  1. Login to SuccessFactors application.
  2. Go to Admin Center-> Import Employee Data and Generate the template for Basic Import.

     

  3. Enter relevant detail for following fields: Status, UserId, Username, FirstName, LastName, Email ID, Manager (as NO_MANAGER ) and HR as (NO_HR) fields
  4. Save the changes.
  5. Go back to Import Employee Data screen and import the csv file for Entity Basic Import after data validation.

     

  6. Access Admin Center > Reset User Password.
  7. Search for the newly created API user and reset the user password.

     

  8. Access Admin Center > Manage Permission Role.
  9. Select an existing Permission Role or create a new one.
  10. Assign Manage Integration Tools > Allow Admin to Access OData API through Basic Authentication and click Save Changes:

     

  11. Assign this permission role to a permission group in SuccessFactors. Ensure the newly created user is also a member of this group.

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hi @graziela @CarlosAccorsi @scar ,

 

For Washington PFML (Family and medical leave) state plan only. please let me know what are all the tax types that needs to be configured.

it should be 087 and 098 only right or even 099 and 100 should be configured for Washington employees?.... need your input

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Activate Employee Central Quick Links
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Activate Employee Central Quick Links

NOTE: Below steps are valid for Employee Central customers only. You may skip this section if not testing the Employee Central specific use cases. 

  1. Go to Admin Center >> Manage Permission Role.
  2. Under Manage Business Configuration >> Employee Central Quick Action Template enable the Visibility and Actions permissions.

     

  3. Save your changes.
  4. Access Admin Center >> Manage Data and confirm Employee Central Quick Action Template objects are visible in the dropdown.

     

  5. If objects are not visible, then you can create the template objects by clicking on Create New and select Employee Central Quick Action Templates.

     

Templates assignments to User Based on which use case's access you would like to assign to the user, you can control this via Role based permission For example: To assign use case View Location, follow below steps:

  1. Go to Admin Center-> Manage Permission Role and open User's role.
  2. Navigate to Employee Central Quick Actions and select the use cse View Location as shown below:

     

  3. Save Changes.

NOTE: Above configuration should allow you to get the response with data while interacting with JOULE.

 

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Register an SAP SuccessFactors System in a BTP Global
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Register an SAP SuccessFactors System in a BTP Global Account

  1. Access BTP Cockpit URL. 
  2. Select the BTP Global Account that has the Joule entitlements and click Continue.

     

  3. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select System Landscape.

     

  4. Click Add System.
  5. Specify a System Name of your choice and select SAP SuccessFactors from the System Type dropdown.

     

  6. Click Get Token.

     

  7. Copy the Registration Token value and share it with your SuccessFactors Admin. Close the token registration screen.

     

  8. Log into SAP SuccessFactors as an admin user and search for Extension Center.

     

     Note: If you don't have access to Extension Center, follow the steps in the preparation section.
  9. Paste the token received from the BTP admin into the Integration Token text box and click Add.

     

  10. Confirm the integration status shows Integrated in SuccessFactors system.

     

  11. Confirm the status is updated to Registered in the BTP Cockpit.

     

     Note: You may have to refresh the BTP Cockpit browser session to see the updated status.

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Re: Can I change associated email address for user id?
entry.content: Thanks!

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Configure Subaccount in SAP BTP for Joule
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Configure Subaccount in SAP BTP for Joule

Create Subaccount and Cloud Foundry Space 

  1. Access BTP Cockpit URL.
  2. Select the BTP Global Account that has the Joule entitlements and click Continue.

     

  3. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Account Explorer. Click Create >> Subaccount.

     

  4. Specify Display Name and Region and click Create.

     


    Note: The Region must be from one of the supported data centers for Joule and correspond to your SuccessFactors tenant. See Data Center Mapping between SAP SuccessFactors and Joule
  5. Click Enable Cloud Foundry.

     

  6. Leave the default settings and click Create.

     

  7. From the Navigation Pane, expand Cloud Foundry and click Spaces.

     

  8. Click Create Space.

     

  9. Specify a Space Name and click Create.

     

Add Entitlements

  1. From the Navigation Pane, select Entitlements and click Edit.

     

  2. Click Add Service Plans.

     

  3. Search for "Joule". Click Joule and choose foundation (Application) from the list of available plans.

     

  4. Search for "workzone". Click SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and choose the foundation and foundation (Application) plans. Click Add 3 Service Plans.

     

  5. Click Save.

     

Add Trusted Domains

  1. From the Navigation Pane, select Settings and click Add.

     

  2. Add your SuccessFactors tenant domain and click Add. For example, https:///hcm-us10.hr.cloud.sap.
    NOTE: Do not add trailing slash(/) at the end of the URL.

     

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Re: How do we get SF to mark the SCORM as complete for pa...
entry.content: Hi PL1 and with @gyoung. Thanks so much for your help.

entry.title: Re: How do we get SF to mark the SCORM as complete for pa...
entry.content: PL1, the item is allocated to learners as part of a Program. The document you sent suggests that setting the 'Upon Failure' action may not work for items assigned in programs. Is that correct? Is there a more another solution? Thanks again

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Establish Trust between Subaccount and SAP IAS
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Establish Trust between Subaccount and SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service

  1. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Trust Configuration. Click Establish Trust.

 

  1. From the list of available SAP Cloud Identity Services tenants, choose the one that is also used by your SuccessFactors tenant and click Next.
    Note: It's important to the choose the correct SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant. The tenant chosen here must be the same as the one used by SuccessFactors instance for which Joule is being configured. Refer to the preparation steps section if unsure of what tenant to select from the list.

     

  2. Choose the appropriate domain for your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant. Select either ias.accounts.ondemand.com or ias.accounts.cloud.sap and click Next.
    Note: Refer to the preparation steps section if you are unsure of which domain to choose.

     

  3. Leave the default settings and click Next.

     

  4. Review the information is correct and click Finish.

     

  5. Confirm the new trust with SAP Cloud Identity Services is visible under Custom Identity Provider for Applications.

     

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Create SAP Build Work Zone Instance
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Create SAP Build Work Zone Instance

Create SAP Build Work Zone Instance and Subscription 

  1. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Instances and Subscriptions. Click Create.

     

  2. Select SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition from Service dropdown and Subscriptions >> foundation from the Plan dropdown. Click Create.

     

  3. Under Instances and Subscriptions click Create button again.
  4. Select SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition from Service dropdown and instances >> foundation from the Plan dropdown.

     

  5. Specify an Instance Name and click Create.

     

  6. Confirm the subscription to the Work Zone and creation of the instance is successful.

     

  7. Click the navigation arrow to view the Work Zone instace details.

     

  8. Under Service Keys click Create.

     

  9. Specify a Service Key Name and click Create.

     

  10. Click Actions icon next to the key and click Download.

     

  11. Confirm the key.txt file is successfully downloaded into your downloads folder.

     

  12. Open the file in a text editor to review its content. Make note of the items in red as they will be required for further configuration.

     

Configure SAP Build Work Zone to use SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication

For more information on this, see Switching to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication

  1. From the Navigation Pane in BTP Cockpit, select Security >> Users and click the arrow to open user details.

     

  2. Scroll down to role collections section and click Additional Details icon >> Assign Role Collections.

     

  3. Select the Launchpad_Admin and click Assign Role Collection.

     

  4. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Instances and Subscriptions*. Click SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition to launch the application.

     

  5. Select Default Identity Provider.

     

  6. Click the Settings icon and select Identity Authentication tab. Make sure the checkbox is selected and click Enable.

     

  7. Confirm the Identity Authentication is enabled successfully.

     

  8. In the BTP cockpit, navigate to your BTP Global Account.

     

  9. Under System Landscape and confirm that you now also see a new system of type SAP Build Work Zone listed as a registered system. This system is automatically added to the System Landscape from the SAP Build Work Zone subscription that you created earlier. Make a note of the System Name for this system as it will come in handy later when executing the Joule booster.

     

    Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Re: ArcGIS license requirement to use GEF in SSAM
entry.content:
Hello @shirsendu_roy2 ,

Here you go:

To activate the map features in SSAM you are exploring two options:

1. ArcGIS: You will require ArcGIS license to integrate it with SSAM.

2. GEF: You will require license from ESRI to use it. ArcGIS license is not required.

But why it is like this. let us understand that.

ESRI is a company and ArcGIS is their product. So when you buy ArcGIS license, actually you are buying ESRI product with license. So you do not have to buy ESRI license separately.

But when you buy SAP GEF, you need to buy ESRI license because SAP GEF integrates with ESRI’s ArcGIS platform to enable geospatial functionality. It is not a product of ESRI but integrates with ESRI. GEF is SAP's standard product so easy to integrate and manage with SSAM. 

ESRI stands for : Environmental Systems Research Institute. 

Ques. Is it possible to use GEF integration with SAP Service and Asset Manager to enable the map functionality without an ArcGIS license? - 
Ans: Yes, but you need ESRI license.

Hope this helps you. Feel free to ask more.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
@Suzanne_Heiob 

I have checked the  alternative certification list and my certification P_SAPEA is listed as "Available Certification".

However, it is not visible on SAP Learning so I cannot access the stay certified assessment.  My universal ID is setup correctly.

Is there an alternative way to get access to the stay certified assessment so it can be completed on-time?

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Run Booster in SAP BTP for Joule
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Run Booster in SAP BTP for Joule

  1. If necessary, navigate to your BTP Global Account.

 

  1. From the Navigation Pane on the left, click Boosters >> search for Joule >> click Start on Setting up Joule tile.

     

  2. Confirm the pre-requisites check is successful and click Next.

     

  3. From the Subaccount dropdown select the Joule subaccount created earlier and click Next.

     

  4. From the Products dropdown select SAP SuccessFactors and choose whether the integration is for Testing or Production. Click Next.

     

  5. Confirm the SAP SuccessFactors is selected from the Capability Pacakages dropdown and click Next.

     

  6. Leave the default Formation Name. From the SAP SuccessFactors System dropdown choose the SuccessFactors system registered under the BTP System Landscape in earlier steps. For SAP Build Work Zone, select the SAP Build Work Zone subscription that you created earlier in BTP subaccount. Similary for SAP Cloud Identity Services system, choose the system registered in BTP Global Account. The SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant chosen here must match what is used by SuccessFactors and what was selected during the setup of the BTP subaccount trust. Click Next.

     

  7. Confirm the validation check is successful and click Next.

     

  8. Review the configuration settings and click Finish.

     

  9. Confirm the booster execution is successful and click Close.

    Update Configuration in SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Update Configuration in SAP IAS
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Update application configuration in SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service 

  1. Access the administration console of SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant using one of the URL formats below:
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.ondemand.com/admin
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.cloud.sap/admin
      Note: Substitute your-ias-tenant with your actual tenant's name.
  1. Authenticate using an administrator user.

     

  2. From the menu, access Applications & Resource >> Applications.

     

  3. Select your SuccessFactors application and click on Conditional Authentication.

     

  4. Validate the Conditional Authentication settings for the application, but do not change anything in the existing setup. The settings here would fall into one of the following 3 scenarios:
  • Scenario 1: SuccessFactors application is setup to use Identity Authentication as the Default Identity Provider
  • Scenario 2: SuccessFactors application is setup to use Identity Authentication as the Default Identity Provider but there are conditional rules setup to delegate authentication to a 3rd party corporate IDP
  • Scenario 3: 3rd party IDP is setup as the Default Identity Provider eg. OKTA in my screenshot below.

     

  1. Switch to the application created for Joule by the BTP booster that was executed earlier. The application name should be with the format das-ias (Name of your subaccount). Set the Conditional Authentication settings for this application to match exactly what was set for the SuccessFactors application. For eg. if the SuccessFactors application is setup with Scenario 3, you must also change Default Identifier in this application accordingly.

     

Update Trusted Domains settings

  1. From the menu, access Applications & Resource >> Tenant Settings.

     

  2. Click Customization >> Trusted Domains.

     

  3. If necessary, click Add to add the domain of your SuccessFactors tenant and click Save. For eg. if your SuccessFactors tenants URL is <--https://hcm-us10.hr.cloud.sap/login?company=yourcompany --> the entry to add is *.hr.cloud.sap.

    Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Re: Transport auf Multi-Target-Applications between Subaccounts
entry.content:
Hi @BorisZarske,

I just came across this post and I am in the same situation, except I would like to ship the app to my customer BTP Global account.

Is there a best practice on how to do this? I explore the Landscape Portal but to my knowledge it applies only to SAP BTP ABAP Environment, right ?

Is the import/export of MTAR files the recommended approach? Does the CTMS is also a good fit for this use case ?

Thanks,

EC

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Create Destinations in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Create Destinations in SAP BTP

  1. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Connectivity >> Destinations. Click Create Destination. 

 

  1. Specify the information below to create the destination and click Save:
    • Name: NavigationService
    • Type: HTTP
    • URL: <--portal-service url from the key file downloaded earlier-->
    • Proxy Type: Internet
    • Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
    • Token Service URL: <--url field from the key file downloaded earlier. Make sure to add /oauth/token to end of the URL-->
    • Token Service URL Type: Dedicated
    • Client ID: <--clientid from key file downloaded earlier-->
    • Client Secret: <--clientsecret from key file downloaded earlier-->
    • Use default JDK truststore: checked

       

  2. Click Create Destinations to create a new destination using the information below and click Save:
    • Name: LPS_SFSF_dt
    • Type: HTTP
    • URL: <-- https://yourSFSFtenantAPIURL/rest/servicesfoundation/sfcdmcontentservice/v1/SFCDMContent (see preparation section to find the API URL for your SuccessFactors instance-->
    • Proxy Type: Internet
    • Authentication: BasicAuthentication
    • User: <-- SAP SuccessFactors username with oData API access and company ID in the format of **username@COMPANYID** (see preparation section for more information on how to create this API User) -->
    • Password: <-- Password for the user above -->
    • Use default JDK truststore: checked
    • New Property >> HTML5.DynamicDestination: true

       

  3. Click the pencil icon to edit the LPS_SFSF_rt destination that is automatically created by the booster you ran earlier.

     

  4. Click New Property and type sap-start. Set the value of of the propery to true and Save the destination.

     


    Important: You may also need to the update the URL parameter in this destination in certain scnearios. For customers whose SuccessFactors tenants are migrated to Common Super Domain(CSD) or access SuccessFactors through a reverse proxy need to update the URL parameter to match to their CSD or reverse proxy URL.
    Note: sap-start must be all in lower case.

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Create Content Provider in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Create Content Provider in SAP Build Work Zone

  1. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Security >> Users and click the arrow to open user details.

 

  1. Scroll down to role collections section and click Additional Details icon >> Assign Role Collections.

     

  2. Select the Launchpad_Admin and click Assign Role Collection.

     

  3. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Instances and Subscriptions*. Click SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition to launch the application.

     

  4. If prompted to choose the authentication provider, click Default Identity Provider.

     

  5. Click Channel Manager icon.

     

  6. Click +New.

     

  7. Add a new content provider using the information below and click Save:
    • Title: <-- Title of your choice eg. **SFSF** -->
    • Design-Time Destination: <-- Design time destination created earlier eg. **LPS_SFSF_dt** -->
    • Runtime Destination: <-- Runtime destination created by the booster eg. **LPS_SFSF_rt** -->
  8. Runtime Destination for Dynamic Data: Use default runtime destination
    Note: Make a note of the ID that is generated automatically on this screen. This ID is required when we setup the user provisioning job to SAP Build Work Zone in later steps.

     

  9. Confirm the content provider is successfully created.

     

    Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Re: Developing with SAP Integration Suite Learning Journe...
entry.content: Hi @HammadSharif1 thanks for your response. It looks like steps 1-3 are fine, and I was able to get the API Proxy URL working in Postman without issue (Step 4). Also in this learning journey's previous exercises we had setup a policy for basic authentication. In regards to the OData Adapter we configure it to have Authentication 'None'. I assume this is ok as these were all instructed steps and I imagine the previously mentioned policy allows for this configuration. In terms of the outcome, I couldn't really identify anything that needed to be changed so the integration flow still fails.

entry.title: Re: Developing with SAP Integration Suite Learning Journey exercises - Error with OData Adapter
entry.content: Hi @BraaivleisEnRugby, thanks for the response. That's interesting, though might prove difficult for me to continue with the exercises then unfortunately.

entry.title: Why opening calculation view leads to OOM error when the calc scenario is based on an empty table?
entry.content:
I have a calculation scenario that is being ran on HANA DB. I can create the calculation scenario. However, if I create a calculation view using the calculation scenario. When we open the calculation view through data preview it would lead to an OOM error even when the source table on top of which the calculation scenario is created is empty.

We get the following error in the HANA console when trying to open the calculation view : could not generate more expressions. Here is link to the faulty calculation scenario (it's too long to be pasted here). Is there a limit to how long a calculation scenario can be ? How can someone go on debugging what's wrong with the following calculation scenario or is there a limitation on hana-db calculation engine.

The hana version is 2.00.059.12.1709045428. The calculation scenario is generated using an internal js library.

https://sap-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/rafid_pranta/EZeI7iMBSf9BrHnPqe1yhuIBd7Zkvie1qXg1K6j6Jd58JA?e=OOiLiD

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Configure IPS for User Replication to Work Zone
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Configure Identity Provisioning Service for User Replication to Work Zone

Configure SuccessFactors as Source System in Identity Provisioning 

  1. Download IdentityProvisioningFiles.zip that contains the pre-defined source and target systems required to setup this configuration.
    Note: A file should be automatically downloaded into your downloads folder.
  2. Extract the zip file into a folder of your choice. Confirm that the following 2 files are visible in the extracted folder:
    • SuccessFactors-SF-Company-ID-Joule.json
    • WorkZone_Target_ForJoule.json

       

  1. Access the administration console of SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant using one of the URL formats below:
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.ondemand.com/admin
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.cloud.sap/admin
      Note: Substitute your-ias-tenant with your actual tenant's name.
  1. Authenticate using an administrator user.

  1. From the menu, access Identity Provisioning >> Source Systems.

     

  2. Under Source Systems click +Add icon.

     

  3. Click Browse to import a pre-defined source system configuration.

     

  4. Select the SuccessFactors-SF-Company-ID-Joule.json file downloaded earlier and click Open.
  5. Update the System Name field and put in your SuccessFactors system company ID and click Save.

     

  6. Click on Properties and replace the placeholder values with appropriate values for your setup using the information below:
    • sf.company.id : Replace with placeholder value with your actual SuccessFactors CompanyID. For e.g. DemoCompany in my screenshot.
    • URL : Specify the API URL corresponding to your SuccessFactors instance. For e.g. https://api8.cert.successfactors.com in my setup. Refer to the preparation steps if unsure of what URL to use.
    • sf.user.filter : Update the userName filter to include your own SuccessFactors users. Use or to add additional users who should be replicated into Work Zone when this job is run later.
      Note: The reason for using a filter for specific users is so that we don't replicate the full SuccessFactors user base into Work Zone. This setup will make it easier to test with few users first before rolling out to larger number of users. The userName part of the filter can be removed later if full user synch is required.

       

  1. Click Save.
  2. Click on Outbound Certificates icon and click Download Certificate icon. This will download the certificate into your downloads folder. Note: This certificate will need to be imported into SAP SuccessFactors system in later steps.

     

Configure SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition as a target system in Identity Provisioning

  1. From the menu, access Identity Provisioning >> Target Systems.

     

  2. Under Target Systems click +Add icon.

     

  3. Click Browse to import a pre-defined target system configuration.
  4. Select the WorkZone_Target_ForJoule.json file downloaded earlier and click Open.

     

  5. From the Source System dropdown make sure to select the source system created earlier. For e.g. SuccessFactors - DemoCompany-Joule in my screenshot.

     

  6. Switch to the Properties tab and update the following placeholders with the appropriate values for your system:
  • cflp.providerId: <-- **ID** of the content provider create in Work Zone. **SFSF** in my case -->
  • URL: <--portal-service url from the key file downloaded earlier when we create Work Zone instance in BTP-->
  • OAuth2TokenServiceURL: <--url field from the key file downloaded earlier. Make sure to add /oauth/token to end of the URL-->
  • User: <--clientid from key file downloaded earlier-->
  • Password: <--clientsecret from key file downloaded earlier-->

     

  1. Click Save.

Import the certificate into SAP SuccessFactors

  1. Log into SAP SuccessFactors using an admin user.
  2. Access Security Center from search area.

     

  3. Click X.509 Certificate Mapping tile.
    Note: If the tile is not visible, make sure you have the appropriate permissions assigned to your SuccessFactors admin user. See preparation section.

     

  4. Click the Add button.

     

  5. Specify the following information and Save your configuration:
  • Configuration Name: <--Name of your choice--->
  • Integration Name: Select Identity Provisioning Service from the dropdown.
  • Certificate File: Upload the certificate file downloaded from the SuccessFactors source system we created in earlier steps.
  • Login Name: <--Leave blank-->

     

  1. Confirm the certificate is successfully uploaded in SuccessFactors.

     

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Add Joule Specific Permission for User in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Add Joule Specific Permission for Users in SAP SuccessFactors

 

  1. In SFSF instance, navigate to Admin Center-> Manage Permission Groups.

 

  1. Click Create New to create a new group for users who will require access to Joule.
  2. Specify a Group Name and click Pick a Category.

     

  3. Search for the username and select the users who should be having JOULE access. Click Done

     

  4. Click Done again to save your changes for the group.

     

  5. Navigate to Admin Center-> Manage Permission Roles.

     

  6. Click Create New. Specify a Role Name and click Permissions:

     

  7. Under General User Permission >> Access to Joule permission and click Done.
    Note: You may need to start a brand new browser session if the permission is not visible.

     

  8. Under Grand this role to.. Click Add and select the permission group created earlier.

     

     

     

  9. Click Done.

     

  10. Click Done again on the next screen.
  11. Click Save Changes.

     

NOTE: Above steps will allow authorized users to access the Joule functionality and based on user's data access for supported use case, information will be available. Also, this permission is only visible after booster execution in BTP subaccount.

 

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Run Refresh SyntheticGroupData Job in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Run Refresh Synthetic Group Data Job in SAP SuccessFactors

  1. Login to SuccessFactors Application. 
  2. Navigate to Admin Center-> Scheduled Job Manager.
  3. Under Job Scheduler tab, click on Create Job Request.

     

  4. Enter the following information:
    • Job Name
    • Job Type: Refresh Synthetic Group Data
    • Job Parameters: Common Data Model Content Deep-Link Access User Group
    • Occurrence: Recurring
    • Recurrence Pattern: Daily
    • Start Date: Use current date and set to execute in few minutes
    • End Date: Time frame of your choice 

       

  5. Click Submit.

Note: This job must be successfully executed once before proceeding to the next step. Set the start time of job to execute so that it executes in the next few minutes.
Note: This job execution would make sure all the users who has been assigned deep link specific access are refreshed and synced correctly during Identity Provisioning sync to SAP Build Work Zone application.

 

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - ProvisionUsersAndGroups from SAP SuccessFactors to WorkZ
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Provision Users and Groups from SAP SuccessFactors to Work Zone

  1. Access the administration console of SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant using one of the URL formats below: 
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.ondemand.com/admin
    • https://your-ias-tenant.accounts.cloud.sap/admin
      Note: Substitute your-ias-tenant with your actual tenant's name.
  2. Authenticate using an administrator user.
  3. From the menu, access Identity Provisioning >> Source Systems.
  4. Under Source Systems select the source system we created earlier for Joule integration and click Job.

     

  5. Click Run Now to run the Read job.

     

  6. From the menu, access Identity Provisioning >> Provisioning Logs.

     

  7. Confirm the job executes successfully.
    Note: It may take few minutes for the job to appear in this interface. The job execution time will also vary depending upon number of users being read. It may take up to 15 minutes to provision few users and groups that meet the filter criteria specified in the job properties. For full user synch it can take several hours.

     

  8. Click the job to view the Job Execution Details and confirm users and groups that meet the filter criteria are created in Work Zone application.

    Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Validate User Provisioning in SAP Build Work Zone, stand
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Validate User Provisioning in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Note: In order to run through these steps, you will need to ensure Launchpad_Admin role is assigned to the user accessing Work Zone. 

  1. Access BTP Cockpit URL.
  2. Select the BTP Global Account that has the Joule entitlements and click Continue.
  3. Select the BTP Subaccount where Joule was setup.
  4. From the Navigation Pane on the left, select Instances and Subscriptions*. Click SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition to launch the application.
  5. If prompted to choose the authentication provider, click Default Identity Provider or your SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service URL.
  6. Under Channel Manager click the Update Content icon.

     

  7. Click Report to see the content was successfully created for the SuccessFactors content provider.

     

  8. Click Settings >> Identity Provisioning. Under Explore Role Assignments section, search for user using their email and click View Assigned Roles.
    Note: The user should have some roles assigned. The Provider id should match what we setup in Work Zone specifically for Joule integration.

    Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

entry.title: Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors HCM - Test Joule in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Back to Main Blog

Test Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

NOTE: To test Employee Central specific use cases you must have Employee Central Quick Links also enabled. See the pre-requisites section for more information on how to enable Employe Central Quick Links. The full list of supported use cases for Joule are accessible from here. 

  1. Login to SuccessFactors application with user who has been granted JOULE access.
  2. Click on JOULE icon visible on home page.

     

  3. This would launch JOULE and you are ready to test the supported use cases available for SuccessFactors.

     

  4. Type View Direct Manager.

     

  5. Enter Mine to view your managers details.

     

  6. Click on this Navigation link to view details of your manager profile.

     

  7. Confirm that you can successfully navigate to page where your managers profile is visible.
  8. Test additional use cases as described in the link above.

Fabian Zapata: Senior Business Process Consultant SAP SuccessFactors HCM | Champion Artificial Intelligence HCM | EmLA Engagement Lead | Visionary Leader LATAM - FRW Future Ready Workforce

I assume that we need Advanced license because Consignment Order is used in the subcontracting scenario and advanced license is necessary to use Consignment Order.

Best regards,

Tatsuya Eda

entry.title: Re: T_code webgui shows error - page can not be displayed
entry.content:
Hi Satish,

As Leo commented, it is suggested to check t-code SOLMAN_SETUP->Infrastructure Preparation->step 2.1 & step 2.2. The webgui is also triggered from the SAP Solution Manager. The URL generation mechanism is read from table HTTPURLLOC. The two configuration steps make sure the correct mechanism. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: How to add sales order types on the pull-down menu in Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app
entry.content:
Dear Experts!

We would like to know if sales order type "CCPU" and "CCRE" can be added to the pull-down menu as below when creating a new sales order in the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app.

Currently, we only see "CCFU" "CCIS" and "OR" only.

We have already confirmed that "CCPU" and "CCRE" is already maintained in the implementation activities but we are not aware of how they can  be reflected in the app.

 

Thank you in advance for your insight!

Fujita

entry.title: Re: Prerequisites and Post steps/checks for Solman SPS up...
entry.content:
Dear Appuru,

For your questions:
Should the CHARM setup be disabled or enabled before starting the SPS upgrade (prerequisite)?
Answer: there is no such prerequisite for CHARM. 

Do we need to re-run every step in Mandatory Configuration after completing the SPS upgrade (post-upgrade)?
Answer: It only needs to execute the steps with yellow exclamatory mark in SOLMAN_SETUP after the upgrade. 

For the pre and post steps for solution manager 7.2 SPS upgrade, it's suggested to read the Release Information Notes. The latest version is 7.2 SP20.

Release Information Note
3471633 SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SP Stack 20: recommended corrections
Central Correction note
3495997 Collective Note SP20: Basic Functions - SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SPS 20

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Query using the service of Northwind of ODATA
entry.content: Hi Emre, I am putting the URL in my Browser.

entry.title: Re: Is it necessary to separate B1i application and datab...

entry.title: Re: Is it necessary to separate B1i application and datab...

For the database, it is just a simple backup and restore to the new server, but I am not sure what related configuration files in B1if need to be modified.

entry.title: Re: UPLOAD_OLE exception thrown when uploading Excel file with Comments
entry.content:
Hi,

Perhaps you can check your file location. Is it under the located at the directory path with too long name. Kindly place the file in shorter directory path location, and this error 2 will be resolved.

I encounter the same and when I move my file to shorter directory path name then it can reach and read the file without error 2 return. 

Hope this helps you.

entry.title: Re: Does sybase databse version 16.0.2.8 support SUSE Lin...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Solution Manager 7.2 supports the SUSE 15 & Sybase 16.0 as shown in the PAM page:
https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/pam?hash=pvnr%3D01200615320900006067%26pt%3Dt%257CPLTFRM%26ainstnr%3D01200615324900011741%26fclfilter%3DG1%257CSAP%2520ASE%2520FOR%2520BUSINESS%2520SUITE%253BG2%257CLINUX%2520ON%2520X86_64

SAP ASE 16.0 FOR BUS. SUITE [>= SP002]
Supported Until
 
31.12.2027
 
: 
 Details
LINUX SUSE SLES15/X86_64 64BIT
Supported Until
 
31.07.2031
 
: 
 Details
SAP KERNEL 7.53 64-BIT UNICODE
Supported Until
 
31.12.2027
 

 

01.01.2019Display (1)
SAP ASE 16.0 FOR BUS. SUITE [>= SP002]
Supported Until
 
31.12.2027
 
: 
 Details
LINUX SUSE SLES15/X86_64 64BIT
Supported Until
 
31.07.2031
 
: 
 Details
SAP KERNEL 7.54 64-BIT UNICODE
Supported Until
 
31.12.2030
 

 

23.12.2022Display (1)

Please also see Sandra's comment. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

The problem you mentioned should be investigated on the solution manager that is affected. These types of issues are best addressed through support cases. I noticed that the case processor has replied. Can you please try the steps and provide an update on the case?

The community is better for How-to questions that can be answered with general information. Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Meets TypeScript: A Smooth Migration Journey with Joule
entry.content:
Great blog

What does it mean for customers using Fiori apps? Does existing Fiori elements will be migrated to Typescripts or only freestyle ones?

entry.title: Re: How to add sales order types on the pull-down menu in...
entry.content:
Dear Fujita,

Hope you doing well!

The SD Document Category is all "C (Order)" for sales document types CCFU, CCIS, and OR.

However, instead of "C (Order)" SD Document Categories are "H (Returns)" or "I (Order Without Charge)" for types CCPU, CCRE.

And that's the reason why these sales document types are not available in the "Manage Sales Orders - Version 2".

It is standard behavior that for sales document types whose SD Document Categories are others other than "C (Order)", it is NOT supported in the "Manage Sales Orders - Version 2" .

Currently, only the document categories which belongs to "C" are supported.

If you would like to use these sales document types when creating sales documents, you are suggested to use the "VA01" .

Hope the message can help.

Best Regards,
Joey

entry.title: 在汇票业务过程中，能否新增汇票种类和票据类型（银承、商承等），系统暂未找到
entry.content: #SAP S4HANA Cloud 、#SAP S4HANA Cloud Public、 #SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition、 #SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance#、在汇票业务过程中，能否新增汇票种类和票据类型（银承、商承等），系统暂未找到，如何新增

entry.title: Re: Billing blocks for cancelled deliveries in S/4 Public...
entry.content:
Hello @Milica3 

It seems that the proper procedure for outbound delivery may not have been followed. To ensure that the canceled outbound delivery number does not appear in the billing due list, Please follow the steps outlined below:

  1. Reverse the Goods Issue using the "Reverse GI - Outbound Delivery" Fiori application.
  2. Select your outbound delivery number and proceed with the reversal.
  3. Verify the billing due list to confirm the update.

You don't need to maintain the billing block in this approach. Additionally, if required, you can delete the outbound delivery number.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant.

entry.title: Need reference article that is linked to all Merchandise category table
entry.content:

Could you please help on Table that gives me information about Merchandise category and linked reference Article to it .

Thanks and Regards

Abhilash.E

entry.title: Re: Getting solr error while doing Full Indexing.
entry.content:
Hi Manohar,

Please try using the URL "https://127.0.0.1:8983/solr" in the Solr indexing configuration. 

Regards, Avees

entry.title: Re: Windows Username automatically populating sapshcut.exe
entry.content:
Hi 

Try to follow the string on Windows OS

start sapshcut -system=SID -client=XXX -desc="should be on logon pad" -user= username -pw=password -maxgui -command=transaction code

BR

SS

entry.title: Issue with PLANT 2 Stock Inclusion in PLANT 1 Shortage Report
entry.content:
This is regarding the shortage report we generate daily and weekly using t-code ZMD61SHORT. During the run on 03-12-2024, we noticed that stock from PLANT 2 was being included in the PLANT 1 shortage, causing confusion and preventing the actual shortage from being visible.

Could anyone please provide clarification on why stock from PLANT 2 is being included in the PLANT 1 shortage report? Need to understand the reason behind this discrepancy so we can accurately identify the actual shortages.

BR,

SRIRAM R

entry.title: System delay and dbtable data governance
entry.content:
Hi SAP


we are analysing our system  delay and dbtable data governance ,and we find some of the table data is large, except the table YFMLT_LOG_XML which is related to customer business,the others dbtable we have no idea.please help us  to analyes the dbtable which are related to  business services and which are related to system logs ,Whether the scale is reasonable and whether the data volume is too large affects the efficiency , and so on what needs to be archived and what are the archive suggestions 


note: you can see the attachment for the screenshot of the base table with a large amount of data  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: System delay and dbtable data governance
entry.content:
Hi ,

 

Could you please check KBA 2388483 to learn about these dbtables?

##2388483 - How-To: Data Management for Technical Tables

If further help is required, this will need to contact the responsible team of the dbtable.

 

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

entry.title: Unable to launch SAPUI5 Task while opening in SAP BTP Workzone My Inbox app.
entry.content:
i,

I've followed all the steps as mentioned in the tutorial as well as pre-requisite tutorials in my BTP trial account (Integrate UI5 task  - https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-integrate-sapui5-task-process.html    )

However, the My Inbox app is not opening the task, although I can see number of tasks on the tile in the workzone site launchpad. refer screenshots 1 and 2.

Also the Process trigger (local copy) app is not loaded which created using the custom SAP UI5 task (workflow UI MTA module) for SBPA approval Process as per the tutorial (Run process from site - https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-run-business-process-business-site.html ). Refer screenshot 3.

error screenshots given below -

1. MyInbox app shows number of Tasks 

2. Error on opening My Inbox app -

3. Error on load in Process trigger App local copy -

Thanks,

Varad

entry.title: MACRO PARA CARGAR UN ARCHIVO PDF EN FORMA MASIVA
entry.content:
hola

Buenas noches

disculpen ....

existira alguna macro que pueda cargar archivos pdf en forma masiva en la transaccion FB03 de forma masiva?

tengo este pero se queda en el cuadro de importacion y de alli no pasa

If Not IsObject(application) Then
Set SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI")
Set application = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
End If
If Not IsObject(connection) Then
Set connection = application.Children(0)
End If
If Not IsObject(session) Then
Set session = connection.Children(0)
End If
If IsObject(WScript) Then
WScript.ConnectObject session, "on"
WScript.ConnectObject application, "on"
End If
session.findById("wnd[0]").maximize
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nfb03"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtRF05L-BELNR").text = "1500028857"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtRF05L-BUKRS").text = "5000"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtRF05L-GJAHR").text = "2024"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[0]").press
session.findById("wnd[0]/titl/shellcont/shell").pressButton "%GOS_TOOLBOX"
session.findById("wnd[0]/shellcont/shell").pressContextButton "CREATE_ATTA"
session.findById("wnd[0]/shellcont/shell").selectContextMenuItem "PCATTA_CREA"

quise cargarlo de esta direccion 

session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/ctxtDY_FILENAME").text = "C:\Users\Luis Lezcano\Documents\01.pdf"

pero nada

agradecere mucho su ayuda

 

gracias

entry.title: Copying condition type from Quotation to Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a scenario where we have a custom condition type ZL03. It is a header condition and we are able to maintain the value in the quotation as expected.

But, when we are trying to create Sales order wrt quotation, this condition type ZL03 is being greyed out in the SO Header level whereas it is having the option to edit in SO item level.

This is not the expected behaviour. We need the option to edit this condition type in SO Header level and grey out in SO item level.

How can this be resolved?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi,

To copy the LMS connection, you need to redo the copy connection workflow. You will find 2 connections available to select.

Note: LMS connection is not standalone. It depends on the employee central connection. It is a best practice to copy them both

thanks

Mahehswar

entry.title: Re: PPDS - Planning with planning material
entry.content: Hi Rupesh can you please explain how to maintain Product hierarchy in PPDS.

entry.title: Re: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What' s New in Q4 2024
entry.content: Hi there, The release Calendar link does not provide release schedule & details for year 2025

entry.title: Has the FUE Audit policy changed?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

As far as I know, FUE Audit is only performed on production systems.

Can you tell me whether DEV and QAS systems are also subject to FUE Audit management?

 

Regards.

entry.title: Re: SAP BRIM Prepayment functionality for Solution quotat...
entry.content:
Hi,

With "Early Billing" feature released in S/4HANA 2023 FPS2, subscription contracts can be billed and invoiced even before the contract start date.

Have a look at the Early Billing Scenario section under Date management in SAP help document of Subscription Order Management Date Management | SAP Help Portal

An early billing date, when assigned to the subscription item in a solution quotation or subscription order, is passed to the CI billing plan as Diff. Request Date which enables BIT request before its valid from. Thanks.

Regards
Deepak

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Order in S4/HANA from SAP IBP
entry.content: For the second question, https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration-for-data-services/help-center-for-sap-cloud-integration-for-data-services/rest-web-service-options is for the reference about Rest WS datastore in CI-DS

entry.title: Production Order Settlement with TECO/DLV Status
entry.content:
We have few production orders that are not yet completely settled. However, WIP is posted into Finance.

Now we have converted their status to TECO yesterday (15.01.2025) and trying to settle them.

We did the WIP / Variance calculation for them again and now running the settlement in CO88 for 12/2024 period.

But the system is not settling anything saying that there is no accrued cost on them for settlement.

When we try the settlement for them for 01/2025, settlement is happening and the variances are shown in the test run.

Why can't we settle them in 12/2024 ? These orders have many transactions posted in 12/2024 period also and logically it need to have settlement in 12/2024 also ?

Are we missing something here ? Please advise.

entry.title: SAP B1 HANA memory issue
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are running on SAP B1 ON HANA With 10.0 Version FP 2111. (Not B1 for Cloud)

We have 10 Users of SAP B1 (2 Prof + 8 Lim Logistics) & we use one block Hana Engine license to use SAP B1 HANA.

We face SLD issue frequently & our cloud partner informs us that HANA ran out of memory so it is the reason for SLD Service failure.

However after restart of services it resumes to function as normal & we will not face any issue for a month(approx) again same happens, Since this happens cyclic, we would like to understand what is the actual root cause.

As from cloud provider we have the root cause as HANA memory is low and need to increase memory to 2 blocks 128 Gb of memory.

We would like to calculate, estimate how much of memory is required for our database

  • We tried calculating based on three ways using below query we got 1.25 GB
with CST AS (
select SCHEMA_NAME,SUM(MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL/1024/1024/1024) as memGB
from M_CS_TABLES
group by SCHEMA_NAME)
select * from CST

order by memGB desc
  • After Backup of HANA database Uncompressed Size (1.12 GB)
  • After Backup of HANA database Compressed Size (511 MB)

Our calculation was very basic, 1.25 GB* 20 times of size = 25 GB

(10x for Size + 10x for operation capacity)

But still we have not reached 64 GB of HANA memory. Can HANA experts provide us the correct guidance pls on extending our HANA memory Block?

Thank you in advance

Best Regards

Anand Ramachandran

SAP HANA  SAP Business One SAP HANA Cloud 

@Laszlo @simon25 @Laszlo_Thoma @Cocquerel @PierreBrothier @pfefferf

entry.title: Re: Problem to connect Business Application Studio to S/4...
entry.content:
Hi @chris_SAP ,

You Can upload the certificate in Developing system server it will work.

 

Thanks and regards,

Deepak

entry.title: Re: Signavio overview user and used licences
entry.content:
Dear Merlezapf ,

It is not possible to directly extract the user information, including licenses, from the SAP Signavio User Management Section. However, you can follow these steps to obtain the required information:

  1. Extract User List Based on Email IDs:
    You can copy the list of all users based on their email IDs from the 'Manage Users & Access Rights' section of Process Manager under 'Users' tab by using the Message button.

     

     


      
     

  2. Manually Add License Information:
    Once the user list is available, you can manually fill in the license information by referring to the details in the User Management Section for the each user.

    Alternatively, if you need this information consolidated in one step, you can request one-time assistance by creating a support ticket by using for 'SAP for ME' service and support section.

    • Component for Support Ticket:
      BPI-SIG-PM-REP

       

      Br,
      Rahul Aryan

entry.title: Re: How to Restrict Copying the data from one BU_GROUP to another BU_GROUP IN SAP MDG BP
entry.content: Hi Derek, I want to skip the credit data from BP copy functionality. Where exactly we can write a code of enhancement ???

entry.title: Re: Form not shown in MyInbox Workzone standard
entry.content:
I have similar problem. The UI for tasks doesn't load on clicking the tile, however number of tasks are shown on the My Inbox tile in the BTP workzone site.

This is my trial account and I've followed all the steps & pre-requisites as per tutorial - https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-sapui5-task-orderapproval.html.

Tried after updating the site settings to disable Asynchronous Loading, but still same error. Furthermore, on testing independently/standalone the Task UI is loaded for the approval process and also can see it My Inbox if opened from Build lobby.

entry.title: Difference between first bin location and default bin location in item master data
entry.content:
Hi Community, 

I am finding it difficult to understand the difference between "first bin location" and "default bin location" which comes under inventory tab in item master data screen, please do tell the difference between them.


Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to expose on-premise SAP B1 service layer with si...
entry.content: Hi EunSeok, I have watched your video tutorial and I was a bit confused on getting the public IP address. our SAP B1 is on a local network, how to get the public IP? thank you

entry.title: Is it possible to have approval workflow for goods receipt?
entry.content: Is it possible to have approval workflow for goods receipt.

entry.title: Re: Accrual Posting Made Simple with SAP Enterprise Service Management (ESM)
entry.content: @pvsbprasad Thank you for a detailed blog. I have a query that does ESM comes as a addon to Service Cloud V2 or do I need another instance of ESM?

entry.title: Re: Custom Caches in Dynatrace Cloud Monitoring
entry.content: Have you had any solutions for that? I also look for a solution.

entry.title: Re: Solution Manager: Job REFRESH_ADMIN_DATA_FROM_SUPPORT...
entry.content:
Good Morning Vera,

I am in continuous contact with the support consultant working on the ticket, but up to this point, not breakthough has been achieved. I know that such problems are handled in the support channels but was looking for perhaps another partner who might have had this problem as well. You never know.

Of course, I asked a question, but if this is not wished in this forum, I will close the question and try my luck in another way. At the moment, the support channel is also not able to supply a solution. 

Best regards,

Kent

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG feature File Download and File Upload.
entry.content:
Hi Swetha,

In File Upload, you have to prepare and load individual files from every single entity, and also language dependent text fields require a separate upload.

Both Material/BP models have too many entities - so file upload/download becomes very time-consuming. A better approach would be to follow the standard mass processing solution given by SAP. 

You can refer to the link here : https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/16148af5-797c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

 

Hope this helps!!

entry.title: Add sales view to BP roles.
entry.content:
Hello,

I have created LSMW object for customer PERS in that How can I add sales view to BP roles.

Best Regards.

entry.title: SAP BTP - Applying Major Updates on Non-Production Subaccount
entry.content:
Hi all,

This is about the Major / Minor Updates / Upgrades that SAP provides on every month / quarterly basis. In order to have a better control and effectively manage the impact of this changes, can you please guide me on the below questions:

* Is there a way to apply the Updates / Releases on Non-Production Subaccount before the scheduled release date?

* Is there  a way to delay a release ? How to notify this to SAP?

entry.title: Can the approval workflow process in ALM be integrate with MyInbox?
entry.content: I would like MyInbox to be a central place for all the approval process. Can the ALM approval workflow process (notifications) integration with MyInbox?

entry.title: Re: Import data from BW Bex query to SAC system
entry.content: I assume you might be using classic account model which doesn't allow you to change existing import job. I would suggest you convert your model to new model type. With new model you can replace data source(with updated BW query) for existing import job and update mapping.

entry.title: Re: error in re init for ODP in BW HANA
entry.content: is this fixed ? we are getting same error

entry.title: Re: How to expose on-premise SAP B1 service layer with si...
entry.content: Hi @pal3kuno, the basic idea is having one small proxy server with public IP address instead of exposing the B1 server itself to the public network to keep the security best practice. I assume you are in the OP environment. then have one additional small server for NGIX as reverse proxy and put it in the DMZ and assign public IP address.

entry.title: Is Universal Worklist (UWL) is being obsoleted or replacing with MyInbox App?
entry.content:
I want to know whatever happen to Universal WorkList (UWL). 

1. Is it obsolete with the HANA S/4 or is being replaced with MyInbox App?

2. Is MyInbox App is now a central place for all workflow notifications?  What cloud production can it be integrated?  I know it will work with HANA S/4, what about other Cloud product such as Ariba, SuccessFactors, Concur, Fieldglass?

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Hi @Ashok_R ,

This add-in is an interesting feature they have introduced. I have tried to integrate with DSP, BW model from SAC to Power-point Addin but the widget quality is poor. The snapshots are unusable as it doesn't contribute the overall meaning of the widget with proper legends and readings as it is appearing for multiple dashboards that I have tried to integrate.

 

As you can see after clicking on edit ✏️ option from selecting widget as we could not select snapshot of "Add widget" as it does not provide any information.

 

 As you can see the widget quality for below chart (in PowerPoint) is poor and blur after adjusting the widget in MS PowerPoint also as compared to above SAC dashboard widget.

 

So, is there any workaround to improve this feature? or any planning to improve quality of widget and selecting same widget as snapshot from "add Widget" option directly?

Also is there any workaround to filter the widget data on add-in as it doesn't support any page filter or any scripted widget filter?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication(IAS) integration with Microsoft Azure Acti
entry.content:
Hi @arpitoberoi ,

I'm unable to proceed due to an error that the clientId is not registered in the tenant. I've followed the exact steps mentioned in this blog. Can you please let me know if I've missed something here?

entry.title: SAP BTP - Applying Major Updates on Non-Production Subaccount
entry.content:
Hi all,

This is about the Major / Minor Updates / Upgrades that SAP provides on every month / quarterly basis. In order to have a better control and effectively manage the impact of this changes, can you please guide me on the below questions:

* Is there a way to apply the Updates / Releases on Non-Production Subaccount before the scheduled release date?

* Is there  a way to delay a release ? How to notify this to SAP?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors_unable to default a HRIS field value a...
entry.content: Hi Sylvia, thanks for the checking this with your colleague and the reply! I had raised a support case with SAP in the past and the processor closed the case saying that this is not supported by the product and that he checked with product experts. However, I've raised a new case now and hoping this gets some solution now. Happy to send you the case number, if this is something you can follow-up too.  Thanks & BR - DRP @S_Strangfeld

entry.title: Re: Error in PGR via Inbound Delivery in S4 HANA for Inte...
entry.content: Yes I was able to solve it , do MIGO with the Inbound Delivery Number
